89) New attestation for OA imtum - In one Cappadocian tablet1 (kt oo/k 6) we come across the following
statements, in which the word imtum may mean "accused of/ murderer and the principal/ guilty". The relevant
passages of the text follow and the text kt n/k 794,7-112 supports us in this respect.
37) i-nu-me da-mu-lú(38) [a DUMU d]A-ur i-na a-li-kà (39) [ú-ma]-ti1-kà i-ni-pu-u-ni (Rev.40)
[hu-lu-]gá-um i-ba-i-ú-ni (41) [a]-Inal da-me-e f-im-tám1 (42) [ta-]dá-na-ni-a-ti-ma ni-du-wa-ak... "when
bloodshed, concerning the citizen(s) of Ashur in your city and country, has been done (40) and there are losses
(41-42) for the bloodshed/violation done you shall give us the principal/the murderer then we shall kill him".
Compare this expression with kt n/k 794,7-11 another treaty agreed between Anatolia and Ashur :
7) ... u-ma (8) da-mu i-na ma-tí-kà (9) i-ta-áb-i da-i-ki (10) lu ta-du-nu-ni-a-ti-ma (11) lu ni-du-ku
"when bloodshed has taken place in your country, you should give us the murderer (so that) we may kill him".
Here d'iku "murderer" alternates with mtum which should mean also murderer, and in both cases they are to
be punished by death penalty. imtum again occurs in kt oo/k 6, 70-75 with the meaning "principal/the
70) u-ma DUMU A-ur DUMU Kà-ni-i (71) i-ta-ah-ba-al-ma a-na ma-tim (72) a-ni-tim i-ta-la-ak a-
pu-hi-5u (73) tá-am-kà-ra-am a-ni-a-am a-hi-a-am (74) ú a-hu-u lá i-sa-bu-tù-ni ha-bu-la-nam (75) §i-im-
tám i-é-e-ú-ni... "If a citizen of Ashur has been indebted to a Kanishian, and has gone to another country, they
shall not seize another merchant, a colleague or his brother in his place, the creditor should look for the
1. kt oo/k 6. This text was presented in Leiden, during the 48th R.A.I.
2. Salih Çeçen - Karl Hecker, "ina mtika eblum" (AOAT 40 (1995) 35 ff.; Emin Bilgiç, "Ebla in Cappadocian
Inscriptions", in: H. Otten - E. Akurgal - H. Ertem - A. Süel (ed.). Hittite and Other Anatolian and Near Eastern Studies in
90) A Neo-Assyrian Text of unknown Provenance : 1 The text is said to have been brought to the museum by
Bektas Gedik and was purchased by the museum's administration and given inv. no A.3660. The exact
provenance is unknown. We note the fact that the text does not bear an eponym, but reveals itself to be a Neo-
Assyrian text dealing with an informal appeal to the governor. Intact. 7.2x3.5x2.2 cm.
